LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT JESSICA LEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 20, 2015 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 24, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED ON THE GROUNDS OF FAILURE TO EXHAUST ARBITRATION REMEDIES AND LACK OF SUBJECT MATTER JURISDICTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THE FILING OF A MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IN ORDER TO CLARIFY WHETHER THE ALLEGEDLY BREACHED CONTRACT IS ORAL OR WRITTEN, AND THE PARTIES TO THE AGREEMENT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF SECURITIES LAWS IN ORDER FOR PLAINTIFF TO CLARIFY THE GROUNDS AND AUTHORITY FOR A PRIVATE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATIONS OF LAW. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/AJR)

ORDER RE DEFENDANT LEE'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDING

LAW AND MOTION, 302, HEARING ON MOTION-PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND MOTION-PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION ARE BOTH DENIED. THE "ARBITRATION AGREEMENT" (ACTUALLY A SENTENCE IN A DOCUMENT CALLED "CORPORATE BOND") PURPORTS TO REQUIRE ARBITRATION BY THE CHINESE GOVERNMENT. THE "AGREEMENT" IS UNCONSCIONABLE AND UNENFORCEABLE FOR SEVERAL REASONS: (1) IT IS TAKE-IT-OR-LEAVE-IT BOILERPLATE; (2) THE TITLE "CORPORATE BOND" IS MISLEADING; (3) THE ARBITRATION RULES WERE NOT ATTACHED OR OTHERWISE PROVIDED; INDEED, DEFENDANT'S PAPERS GIVE NO INDICATION THAT EVEN HER COUNSEL KNOWS WHAT THE RULES ARE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, MOTION FOR ORDER TO STAY PROCEEDINGS IN PENDING ACTION UNTIL ARBITRATION COMPLETED ARE BOTH DENIED. THE "ARBITRATION AGREEMENT" (ACTUALLY A SENTENCE IN A DOCUMENT CALLED "CORPORATE BOND") PURPORTS TO REQUIRE ARBITRATION BY THE CHINESE GOVERNMENT. THE "AGREEMENT" IS UNCONSCIONABLE AND UNENFORCEABLE FOR SEVERAL REASONS: (1) IT IS TAKE-IT-OR-LEAVE-IT BOILERPLATE; (2) THE TITLE "CORPORATE BOND" IS MISLEADING; (3) THE ARBITRATION RULES WERE NOT ATTACHED OR OTHERWISE PROVIDED; INDEED, DEFENDANT'S PAPERS GIVE NO INDICATION THAT EVEN HER COUNSEL KNOWS WHAT THE RULES ARE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, . DEFENDANT JESSICA LEE'S DEMURRER TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION OF SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AND THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SUFFICIENTLY COMPLIED WITH THE ORDER OF THE COURT TO CLARIFY WHETHER THE ALLEGEDLY BREACHED CONTRACT IS ORAL OR WRITTEN. (SAC PARAGRAPHS 18-22.) PLAINTIFFS REFER TO THE CONTRACT WHICH PROVIDES THE BASIS FOR THE CAUSE OF ACTION AS BOTH AN ORAL AND A WRITTEN CONTRACT. (SAC PARAGRAPH 19.) THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE THE COURT PREVIOUSLY SUSTAINED THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/EG)
ORDER RE:DEFENDANT JESSICA H. LEE'S DEMURRER TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION OF SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald St. Clair of Law Offices of Ronald P. St. Clair telephone number 415-788-1708 for Defendant.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JESSICA LEE'S DEMURRER TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION OF SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained without leave to amend and the motion to strike the third cause of action is granted. The second cause of action is sustained without leave to amend because Plaintiffs have not sufficiently complied with the order of the Court to clarify whether the allegedly breached contract is oral or written. (SAC paragraphs 18-22.) Plaintiffs refer to the contract which provides the basis for the cause of action as both an oral and a written contract. (SAC paragraph 19.) The court grants the motion to strike the third cause of action because the court previously sustained the demurrer to the third cause of action without leave to amend. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Continued To Feb-24-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-21-2015 Continued To Oct-28-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ronald P. St. Clair, of Law Offices of Ronald P. St. Clair, Tel. 415.788-1708, for defendants;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JESSICA LEE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. The elements that must be pleaded under a fraud cause of action are: "(a) misrepresentation (false representation, concealment or nondisclosure); "(b) knowledge of falsity (or 'scienter'); "(c) intent to defraud, i.e., to induce reliance; "(d) justifiable reliance; and "(e) resulting damage." [See Philipson & Simon v. Gulsvig (2007) 154 CA4th 347, 363.] Here, Plaintiffs have sufficiently plead that Defendant Lee misrepresented, with knowledge of falsity and intent to defraud Plaintiffs, Plaintiff's relied on Defendant Lee's misrepresentation, and were harmed by the loss of their investment. Therefore the demurrer to the first cause of action for fraud is overruled.

The Demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for breach of contract. As previously ordered Plaintiffs must clarify whether the allegedly breached contract is oral or written, and the parties to the agreement.

The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for violations of security law. Plaintiffs have amended the third cause of action to specify that the security law allegedly violated by Defendant Lee is Corporations Code 25210 concerning dealer-brokers. However, Plaintiffs have not alleged that Defendant Lee was or is a dealer-broker nor that she ever represented herself to be so. Plaintiffs have not sufficiently complied with the order of the Court to clarify proper grounds and authority for a private cause of actions for violations of security law. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-06-2015 Continued To Jun-03-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Hearing On Motion-Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion-Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Apr-10-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. GERARD LAM, Tel No. (510) 465-6685 appearing for Plaintiffs;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT JESSICA LEE'S Petition And Motion to Compel Arbitration, the motions to compel arbitration and stay proceedings are both DENIED. The "arbitration agreement" (actually a sentence in a document called "Corporate Bond") purports to require arbitration by the Chinese government. The "agreement" is unconscionable and unenforceable for several reasons: (1) it is take-it-or-leave-it boilerplate; (2) the title "Corporate Bond" is misleading; (3) the arbitration rules were not attached or otherwise provided; indeed, defendant's papers give no indication that even her counsel knows what the rules are. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/RBU)

Motion For Order To Stay Proceedings In Pending Action Until Arbitration Completed
Off Calendar Apr-10-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. GERARD LAM, Tel No. (510) 465-6685 appearing for Plaintiffs;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT JESSICA LEE'S Motion For Order To Stay Proceedings In Pending Action Until Arbitration Completed, the motions to compel arbitration and stay proceedings are both DENIED. The "arbitration agreement" (actually a sentence in a document called "Corporate Bond") purports to require arbitration by the Chinese government. The "agreement" is unconscionable and unenforceable for several reasons: (1) it is take-it-or-leave-it boilerplate; (2) the title "Corporate Bond" is misleading; (3) the arbitration rules were not attached or otherwise provided; indeed, defendant's papers give no indication that even her counsel knows what the rules are. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-02-2015 Continued To Apr-22-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-20-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RONALD P. ST. CLAIR, Tel No. (415) 788-1708 appearing for Defendant Jessica Lee;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JESSICA LEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. The Demurrer is overruled on the grounds of failure to exhaust arbitration remedies and lack of subject matter jurisdiction without prejudice to the filing of a motion to compel arbitration. The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for breach of contract in order to clarify whether the allegedly breached contract is oral or written, and the parties to the agreement. The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for breach of securities laws in order for Plaintiff to clarify the grounds and authority for a private cause of action for violations of law. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-24-2014 Continued To Jan-20-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JESSICA LEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to January 20, 2015 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than December 24, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)
